figures.caption: Figure 8: Dual-level evidence access in RETOOLVIDEO: the planner can use both preprocessed structured evidence and raw visual or video evidence returned by tools.

Table 1: Representative video-agent tool libraries compared by tool granularity, functionality, and organization. ✓and ×denote supported and unsupported features, respectively. N/R denotes that the number of tools is not reported.
Table 2: Main results comparing RETOOL-VIDEO with representative large video language models.
Figure 5: Distribution of runtime tool calls in RETOOL-VIDEO, showing the relative invocation frequency of each base/meta tool category during model inference.
Figure 6: Relationship between final accuracy and tool-use behavior, including tool-call count, reasoning iterations, and tool-call success rate.
Figure 7: Case study experiment on MLVU benchmark with RETOOL-VIDEO.
Figure 4: An example of recursive tool grounding in RETOOL-VIDEO, where abstract actions are recursively resolved into executable tool chains.

Figure 3: Overall framework of RETOOL-VIDEO, where the planner selects primitive or abstract actions, the MetaAug-Video tool library provides base and meta tools, and the resolver recursively grounds abstract actions into executable tool chains.
